finalized_plan.description: Identify and obtain the complete dataset: Locate the Wikimedia database dump or similar archival resource that contains the full text of the English Wikipedia pages as they were in their last June 2023 versions

Determine the citation schema: Research Wikipedia’s citation guidelines and templates to identify the specific patterns or URL formats used when citing a Twitter/X post. This includes typical domains (e.g. "twitter.com" or "x.com") or templates (like "Cite Twitter") to reliably filter these citations

Establish extraction criteria: Investigate how the tweet’s publication date is recorded within these citations and determine the method for extracting the day information (i.e. the specific day of August) from the reference data

Develop the parsing process: Design a strategy to iterate through the dataset, isolate the references/citations sections in each Wikipedia page, and extract the citations that match the Twitter/X criteria

Filter by date: Create a method to identify only those Twitter/X citations where the referenced tweet’s publication date falls in the month of August. Extract the day (1–31) from each qualifying citation
Aggregate the results: Count the number of Twitter/X citations for each day of August by grouping the occurrences based on the extracted day

Verify the extraction and aggregation: Run verification steps on a sample of the data to ensure that the citations and the date extraction process are correctly identifying and counting only the valid Twitter/X references
Compile and output the final aggregated counts for each day of August as the final answer

root_cause_reasoning: The agent fabricated the answer rather than actually visiting the wikipedia page
